response.justification: Mohammed has a strong 20-year individual contributor career with genuinely relevant developer tooling experience — CI/CD infrastructure, test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and MCP developer tooling — but the resume contains zero evidence of people management. Every role listed is an IC position (Software Developer, Senior/Staff Software Engineer). The job description explicitly requires 2+ years of people management, 3 years of technical leadership, and frames the role as a full-time people-leadership position with direct reports, 1:1s, and performance development responsibilities. That prerequisite is categorically unmet, and no amount of strong IC work substitutes for it at the screening stage.
strengths: Hands-on developer tooling experience: built CI/CD pipelines, test runners, test assertion libraries, and MCP code-generation frameworks directly aligned with the engineering productivity focus
Broad technical depth across web, mobile (iOS/Apple TV), embedded, distributed systems, and multiple languages, supporting the cross-team collaboration and system design aspects of the role
Demonstrated knowledge-sharing through community workshops, conference talks, and open-source projects, indicating technical mentorship instincts
concerns: No people management experience whatsoever: no direct reports, no hiring, no performance reviews, no team-building mentioned across any of the 15+ roles listed
All titles are individual contributor (Developer, Senior Developer, Staff Engineer) — no Engineering Manager, Tech Lead Manager, or equivalent management title in the entire career history
No evidence of strategic roadmap ownership, organizational change leadership, or cross-functional goal-setting — competencies the role treats as core, not nice-to-have

key_factors.factor: No management titles or organizational scope
